title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Ignore Missing Fields

statement.markdown: Given a MongoDB query and a projection, implement a function that formats the query to ignore missing fields. The input query is a dictionary where each key is a field name and each value is a boolean indicating whether the field should be included in the result. The input projection is also a dictionary with the same structure. Your function should return a new dictionary representing the formatted query.
statement.inputFormat: Query: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: true } Projection: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: null }
statement.outputFormat: Formatted Query: { field1: true, field4: null }
statement.notes: Note that if a field is missing from the query, it should be ignored in the formatted query. Also, if a field is null in the projection, it should be removed from the formatted query.

constraints.description: Maximum number of fields in the query
Maximum number of fields in the projection

examples.input: Query: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: true } Projection: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: null }
Query: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: true, field4: false } Projection: { field1: true, field2: null, field3: null, field4: true }

hints.text: First hint text: Iterate over the query and projection dictionaries to build the formatted query

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Use a dictionary comprehension to create the formatted query

functionSignature.python: def formatQuery(self, query: dict, projection: dict) -> dict:

testCases.input: Query: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: true } Projection: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: null }
Query: { field1: true, field2: false, field3: true, field4: false } Projection: { field1: true, field2: null, field3: null, field4: true }
